BASEBALL;9th-Inning Comeback Falls Short For Mets
nytimes_articles.abstract:
If Coors Field is a hitter's park, then somebody forgot to tell the Mets until it was too late. While the Colorado Rockies used back-to-back home runs by Larry Walker and Andres Galarraga to fuel their offense, the Mets waited until the ninth inning to put a decent rally together and suffered a 6-5 loss for their sixth defeat in their last seven games. 

     Trailing by 6-2 entering the ninph, the Mets came up with three runs on three hits to put a scare into the Rockies. With the bases loaded and two out, reliever Curtis Leskanic struck out Bernard Gilkey.

Make Brooklyn a World-Class Seaport
nytimes_articles.abstract:
The Port of New York and New Jersey has a lemon on its hands. Millions of cubic yards of riverborne mud contaminated by toxins are clogging New York Harbor's channels and docks. Some large ships cannot enter fully loaded and must put part of their cargo on barges first. 

     Solving this problem is not simple. For years, the Army Corps of Engineers routinely dredged the mud and dumped it in the Atlantic six miles east of the Jersey Shore. That is now against Federal law because the mud harms marine life.

China's Europe Card
nytimes_articles.abstract:
Prime Minister Li Peng of China and his Trade Minister, Wu Yi, flew to France last week to sign a $1.5 billion deal to buy 10 passenger planes from Airbus Industrie. The purchase, the largest yet for Airbus, a European consortium, follows Ms. Wu's postponement last month of a trip to the United States, where she was expected to announce a $4 billion order from Boeing and McDonnell-Douglas. 

     Ms. Wu changed her travel plans because the Clinton Administration rightly denied her a meeting with the President and refused to delay imposing sanctions  on China for its intellectual property violations.

CUNY 'Vocational' Friction Has National Echo
New York City Can't Afford This Tax
nytimes_articles.abstract:
Every three months, I make out a check to New York City for, well, never mind the exact number -- suffice to say it's 4 percent of my quarterly earnings. 

     If you get a paycheck, an annual Christmas party and will send off your annual check to the Internal Revenue Service on Monday, you probably have no idea what I'm talking about. But if you are self-employed and a full- or part-time New York City resident you, too, probably pay 4 percent on top of the  taxes everyone else pays. It is called the unincorporated business tax, and it is especially harmful in a city that depends on its freelance writers and artists.

wiki_events.headline: Two women and four children are killed after Israeli helicopter fired rockets at an ambulance in Mansouri, Lebanon.

pages.title: Mansouri attack
pages.abstract: The Mansouri attack occurred on 13 April 1996, when an Israel Defence Forces helicopter attacked an ambulance in Mansouri, a village in Southern Lebanon, killing two women and four children.

pages.title: Al-Mansouri
pages.abstract: Al-Mansouri is a municipality in the Tyre District in South Lebanon.

nytimes_articles.headline: Classic Parsley Potatoes
Artichokes in Parsley Sauce
Salmon in Parsley Sauce
nytimes_articles.abstract: Parsley is the herb taken for granted. A few sprigs solve the garnish problem. A scattering of chopped leaves enlivens almost any dish with a bright bit of green. But parsley can take a more assertive role, becoming the primary component of a sauce. Here, flat-leaf parsley is combined with capers, scallions and garlic to make a sharp, verdant sauce for salmon. It contrasts perfectly with the richness of the fish.
